acceptance_targets: [CH16_CRAFT_DISCOVERY_HANDOUT] By the time the envelope is opened in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:145-173, a cold reader can state why the study is still unprocessed, why no deputy or tech stops Joel and Paul, and what specific cost they risk by touching the document. The revision fails if the safe still appears effectively unguarded simply because the chapter needs Joel and Paul to get the answer first.

[CH16_CRAFT_REVEAL_UNDEREMBODIED] In the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:195-239, the scene must include at least one concrete beat that makes Ray Toomey's death and Martin's long protection of Tommy register as human consequence rather than only motive. A reader should be able to point to a specific sentence or action that lands that cost before the dialogue shifts back to Cliff's phone, Mallick, or what the document proves.

[FIRE_DOC_CONTRADICTIONS_chapter_16] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4080 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.

[SAFE_CONTENTS_PHYSICAL_chapter_16] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4065 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.
[R22] Ch16 contains no more than 2 instances of 'something.'
[R24] Ch16 does not quote more than 2-3 sentences of the fire document. Joel's reading is conveyed through reaction and selective excerpt rather than full reproduction.
[C01] The fire document text in Ch8 and Ch16 matches on: date written, date of fire, structure involved, cause of fire, location of Toomey, and fire ruling.
[length_chapter_16_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: By the time the envelope is opened in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:145-173, a cold reader can state why the study is still unprocessed, why no deputy or tech stops Joel and Paul, and what specific cost they risk by touching the document. The revision fails if the safe still appears effectively unguarded simply because the chapter needs Joel and Paul to get the answer first.
findings.problem: The chapter explains why Mallick has not opened the safe yet, but it does not make Joel and Paul's access feel earned inside an active homicide scene. Paul explicitly says the safe is part of the murder scene, then the two of them simply walk into the study, open the safe, handle the envelope, and read the chapter-defining document before the police get there. Because the page does not establish a concrete barrier, witness gap, or consequence for contaminating evidence, the reveal reads like a plot handout more than a risky civilian discovery.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the access logic around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:35 and the study sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:101-145. Establish exactly why the room is still open to them, what physical or social boundary they cross to get in, and what immediate consequence Joel and Paul risk if Mallick or a BCA tech catches them handling the RT envelope. If the scene keeps the ten-minute window, make that window operationally dangerous rather than merely convenient, and let Joel's legal brain or Paul's procedural caution register the contamination stakes on-page.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passage spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:195-239, the scene must include at least one concrete beat that makes Ray Toomey's death and Martin's long protection of Tommy register as human consequence rather than only motive. A reader should be able to point to a specific sentence or action that lands that cost before the dialogue shifts back to Cliff's phone, Mallick, or what the document proves.
findings.problem: The RT document contains the chapter's ugliest moral material, but the aftermath converts it into leverage math almost immediately. Ray Toomey arrives as age, job title, and motive infrastructure; Martin's twenty-two-year concealment becomes chiefly an explanation for the will and Cliff's leverage play. That keeps the deduction legible, but it makes the buried death and Martin's complicity land thinner than the style bible's demand for embodied guilt and consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Deepen the immediate post-reading beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:195-221 before the conversation narrows to phones, motive, and sufficiency. Give Joel or Paul a concrete, character-true registration of Ray Toomey as a dead man and of Martin's choice as lived damage: sensory residue from the document, an involuntary image, a physical recoil, or a memory-pressure beat that makes the human cost hit the room before the legal/case logic takes over.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4080 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.

acceptance_test.excerpt: 4080: Joel took the mug. Too hot. Too weak. Paul had been making the same bad coffee for twenty-five years and still hadn't improved.

evidence.excerpt: 4080: Joel took the mug. Too hot. Too weak. Paul had been making the same bad coffee for twenty-five years and still hadn't improved.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4080: Joel took the mug. Too hot. Too weak. Paul had been making the same bad coffee for twenty-five years and still hadn't improved.

findings.problem: Joel reads the same physical RT document Cliff read in chapter 8, but now it is dated Aug 14 2002, three sheets, gasoline can near space heater, shed fire (not cabin). The document cannot have changed between readings.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4080. Establish one canonical version of the 2002 fire (date, accelerant, structure, victim location, document page count) and enforce it wherever the RT document is read on-page (chapters 8 and 16 must match exactly). Tommy's memory (chapter 12) and confession (chapter 17) may diverge from the document if the divergence is intentional and flagged as unreliable narration, but the two on-page readings of the same physical document must be identical.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4065 now follows the rewrite direction and no longer exhibits the described defect. Fail if a cold reader would still identify the same underlying problem in those cited passages.
findings.problem: Joel opens the safe: 'yellow RT envelope,' document now 'three sheets.' Envelope color and document page count differ from both prior descriptions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4050; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4065. Fix one canonical description of the safe contents (cash amount, envelope size and color, document page count) and use it in every scene where the safe is opened. If cash is removed between scenes, show it on-page or reference it. The envelope and document are the same physical objects and must match across all observations.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch16 contains no more than 2 instances of 'something.'
findings.evidence: 'Something' appears 108 times manuscript-wide (1 per 604 words). Ch16 contains 6 instances in 2,640 words (1 per 440 words).
findings.problem: Ch16 uses 'something' at 1 per 440 words, well above the 1-per-800 threshold. In Joel's deduction chapter, the hedge weakens the logical precision that defines his voice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Ch16 to no more than 2 instances. Replace with specific nouns or observations.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch16 does not quote more than 2-3 sentences of the fire document. Joel's reading is conveyed through reaction and selective excerpt rather than full reproduction.
findings.evidence: Ch8/2044 and Ch16/4240 both reproduce Martin's fire document opening: 'I'm writing this down because somebody should, and because I don't trust myself to remember it right if I don't write it now while it's still clear.' The opening sentence is verbatim across chapters because it is the same document. However, the document content then diverges significantly (see consistency finding C01).
findings.problem: Martin's fire document is reproduced at length in both Ch8 (lines 2042-2066, ~600 words) and Ch16 (lines 4238-4256, ~500 words). Even accounting for it being the same document, the second full reproduction is a near-verbatim passage that the reader has already absorbed. Ch8 establishes the document; Ch16 should not re-quote it in full.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch16, do not reproduce the full document text. Joel can read silently; the chapter can excerpt only the details NEW to Joel (or that hit differently from his perspective) and summarize the rest in a sentence. The opening line need not be repeated.

findings.acceptance_test: The fire document text in Ch8 and Ch16 matches on: date written, date of fire, structure involved, cause of fire, location of Toomey, and fire ruling.
findings.evidence: Ch8/2042 dates the document 'August 17, 2002' and describes the fire on 'the night of August 11, 2002.' Ch16/4238 dates the same document 'August 14, 2002' with no specific fire-night date. Ch8: kerosene heater knocked over in cabin kitchen, Toomey in back bedroom, fire ruled 'accidental/faulty heater.' Ch16: gasoline can near space heater in 'the shed,' Toomey in the shed, fire ruled 'electrical.' Ch8: Martin drove back alone at 3 AM to reposition heater. Ch16: Martin 'didn't tell the truth in any useful way' but no return trip described.
findings.problem: Martin's fire document is the same physical document read by Cliff in Ch8 and Joel in Ch16, but the two chapters present it with different dates, different fire causes, different structures (cabin kitchen vs. shed), a different location for the victim (back bedroom vs. shed), and a different fire ruling (accidental/faulty heater vs. electrical). These cannot be perspective differences — the document is one artifact.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile the fire document to a single consistent version. Choose one date, one cause, one structure, one fire ruling, and one version of Martin's role. Propagate the chosen version to both Ch8 and Ch16. The Ch12 Tommy-memory version may intentionally diverge (unreliable memory), but the written document must be internally consistent.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
